_meta.description: Presidential pardons and commutations, with documented payment or financial connections to the pardoner where applicable. Distinct from Foreign Ties (which tracks money flowing TO the official); this section tracks the official's exercise of clemency power.

fields.name: Recipient of pardon/commutation
fields.date: Date of pardon (YYYY-MM-DD or 'YYYY-MM') or mass-pardon date
fields.crime: Federal offense(s) they were charged or convicted of
fields.payment_connection: Documented financial relationship to the pardoner
fields.payment_amount: Dollar value of payment connection, or 'None documented'

Donald Trump.name: Trevor Milton
Donald Trump.crime: Securities fraud, wire fraud (Nikola electric truck scheme)
Donald Trump.sentence: 4 years prison, $1M fine, ~$680M in pending restitution to defrauded investors
Donald Trump.payment_connection: Milton and his wife donated to Trump's 2024 reelection effort one month before the election. Total to Trump-aligned vehicles: $3.2M+. Represented by Brad Bondi, brother of Trump AG Pam Bondi.
Donald Trump.summary: Founded Nikola, the EV truck company, then convicted in 2022 of misleading investors about a non-functional prototype that was actually a truck rolling down a hill. Sentenced to 4 years and ~$680M in pending restitution. Trump called Milton personally to inform him of the pardon. Restitution and forfeitures were also wiped, costing defrauded investors hundreds of millions.
Donald Trump.source: Axios, New York Times, The Hill, AP

Donald Trump.name: Changpeng 'CZ' Zhao
Donald Trump.crime: Failing to maintain anti-money laundering program at Binance (Bank Secrecy Act violation)
Donald Trump.sentence: 4 months prison (served), $50M personal fine, $4.3B Binance corporate settlement
Donald Trump.payment_connection: Binance handled MGX's $2B USD1 stablecoin transaction enriching Trump's World Liberty Financial. WLF earns ~$80M/year from the resulting USD1 reserves Binance holds. Binance later expanded USD1 trading pairs after the deal.
Donald Trump.summary: Founded Binance, the world's largest crypto exchange. Pleaded guilty in 2023; DOJ called Binance 'a central hub for terrorists, hackers and human traffickers.' Trump pardon came 6 months after Binance handled the $2B UAE-Trump stablecoin deal. Trump told 60 Minutes he didn't know Zhao but he was 'recommended by a lot of people.'
Donald Trump.source: Wall Street Journal, NBC News, CBS 60 Minutes

Donald Trump.name: Julio Herrera Velutini
Donald Trump.crime: Bribery and corruption (paid $300K to former Puerto Rico Gov. Wanda Vázquez to fire her financial regulator, who was investigating Velutini's bank)
Donald Trump.sentence: Reduced from felony bribery to misdemeanor via plea deal in 2025; pre-sentencing pardon eliminated all consequences
Donald Trump.payment_connection: Daughter Isabela Herrera donated $3.5M to MAGA Inc. between Dec 2024 and July 2025. Campaign Legal Center filed FEC complaint alleging illegal foreign straw-donor scheme; Isabela self-reported as 'self-employed' from a rented residence. Represented by Christopher Kise, Trump's former personal attorney.
Donald Trump.summary: Venezuelan-Italian billionaire banker who founded Bancredito International Bank. Charged in 2022 with bribing the governor of Puerto Rico in exchange for replacing the regulator investigating his ~$10B in transactions. Pardoned alongside the former governor and her former FBI-agent co-conspirator. Foreign nationals are barred from US political contributions.
Donald Trump.source: Bloomberg, New York Times, Campaign Legal Center, Forbes

Donald Trump.name: Imaad Shah Zuberi
Donald Trump.type: pardon (second term, building on first-term commutation)
Donald Trump.crime: Foreign lobbying violations, tax evasion, campaign finance violations (funneled foreign money into US elections)
Donald Trump.sentence: 12 years prison, $15.7M in fines and restitution
Donald Trump.payment_connection: Donated $900K to Trump's 2017 inaugural committee. His original sentence had been commuted in Trump's first term in 2021.
Donald Trump.summary: Venture capitalist who pleaded guilty to funneling foreign money into US political campaigns. His original 12-year sentence was commuted by Trump in 2021; full pardon followed in the second term.

Donald Trump.name: Wanda Vázquez Garced
Donald Trump.crime: Bribery (accepted $300K in campaign contributions from Velutini in exchange for firing a financial regulator)
Donald Trump.sentence: Federal prosecutors had requested 12 months prison
Donald Trump.payment_connection: Endorsed Trump in 2020 reelection. Defended by Christopher Kise, Trump's former personal attorney. Pardoned as part of the same package as her alleged briber (Velutini).
Donald Trump.summary: Former governor of Puerto Rico, pardoned alongside Julio Herrera Velutini, the billionaire who allegedly bribed her. Pre-sentencing pardon eliminated all consequences. The pardon initially failed to include all of her criminal cases; the White House announced a fix.

Donald Trump.name: Henry Cuellar (sitting US Representative, D-TX)
Donald Trump.crime: Federal bribery and money laundering (indicted 2024 for accepting bribes from Azerbaijan and a Mexican bank)
Donald Trump.payment_connection: None documented financially. Cuellar is a conservative Democrat. Days after the pardon he filed to run for reelection as a Democrat, reportedly angering Trump.
Donald Trump.summary: First sitting member of Congress pardoned by Trump in his second term. Cuellar was indicted on bribery and conspiracy charges in 2024. The pardon effectively ended the federal case against him and his wife.

Donald Trump.name: Juan Orlando Hernández
Donald Trump.crime: Drug trafficking conspiracy (involved in moving 400+ tons of cocaine into the US)
Donald Trump.payment_connection: None documented financially. Pardon came amid Trump administration's aggressive Latin American anti-drug-trafficking enforcement.
Donald Trump.summary: Former president of Honduras (2014-2022). Convicted by US jury in March 2024 of running a drug trafficking operation while in office. The pardon released him just days after Honduras's presidential election. Trump defended the pardon saying Hondurans believed Hernández had been 'set up.'

Donald Trump.name: Devon Archer
Donald Trump.crime: Wire fraud, securities fraud ($60M scheme defrauding a Native American tribe)
Donald Trump.payment_connection: Became a key congressional witness in the Republican-led inquiry into Hunter Biden, testifying that Hunter sold 'the illusion of access' to his father.
Donald Trump.payment_amount: None documented financially; political utility documented
Donald Trump.summary: Former business associate of Hunter Biden, convicted in 2022 of a bond fraud scheme against the Oglala Sioux Tribe. Trump said Archer had been treated 'very unfairly.'

Donald Trump.name: Ross Ulbricht
Donald Trump.crime: Operating Silk Road dark-web drug marketplace, money laundering, computer hacking
Donald Trump.payment_connection: Trump promised to pardon Ulbricht during May 2024 speech at the Libertarian Party National Convention as part of his courtship of libertarian voters.
Donald Trump.summary: Founder of Silk Road, the underground marketplace for drugs and illegal goods that operated 2011-2013. Trump pardoned him on the first full day of his second term, fulfilling a campaign promise to libertarian voters.

Donald Trump.name: Rod Blagojevich
Donald Trump.crime: Public corruption, attempted extortion (tried to sell Obama's vacated Senate seat)
Donald Trump.sentence: 14 years (commuted to time served at 8 years in Trump's first term)
Donald Trump.payment_connection: Vocal Trump supporter and self-described 'Trump-ocrat.' Trump commuted his sentence in his first term; full pardon in second term.
Donald Trump.summary: Former Democratic governor of Illinois, impeached and convicted in 2011 of trying to sell President Obama's vacated Senate seat. The 2025 pardon erases his criminal record entirely.

Donald Trump.name: George Santos
Donald Trump.payment_connection: None documented financially. Trump-aligned former Republican Representative who was expelled from the House.
Donald Trump.summary: Disgraced former US Representative (R-NY) expelled from the House in 2023. Pleaded guilty to wire fraud and identity theft. Trump commuted his sentence in October 2025.

Donald Trump.name: January 6 Capitol attackers (mass pardon)
Donald Trump.crime: Various offenses related to the January 6, 2021 Capitol attack including violent assault on police officers, seditious conspiracy
Donald Trump.sentence: Approximately 1,500 individual sentences wiped; 14 commutations for far-right leaders including Enrique Tarrio (22 yrs, Proud Boys) and Stewart Rhodes (18 yrs, Oath Keepers)
Donald Trump.payment_connection: Political: fulfillment of campaign promise. Many recipients had organized to support Trump's 2020 election denial.
Donald Trump.summary: Trump's first-day mass clemency for approximately 1,500 people charged in connection with the January 6, 2021 Capitol attack, including those convicted of violent assault on police officers. Pardon Attorney Liz Oyer was fired in March 2025 after refusing to recommend restoration of gun rights for Trump ally Mel Gibson.

Donald Trump.name: Fake electors (77 people)
Donald Trump.crime: Various offenses related to the 2020 fake electors plot to overturn the presidential election. Recipients included Rudy Giuliani, Mark Meadows, John Eastman, and others.
Donald Trump.sentence: Preemptive (none facing federal charges at time of pardon). State charges unaffected.
Donald Trump.payment_connection: Political: protected Trump's own allies and former aides from future federal prosecution related to overturning his election loss.
Donald Trump.summary: Mass preemptive pardon covering 77 people involved in the 2020 fake electors scheme. None faced federal charges at the time. The proclamation's broad language may also apply to others, including the 2025 DNC/RNC pipe bomber suspect according to one former DOJ prosecutor.

Donald Trump.name: Brian Kelsey
Donald Trump.payment_connection: Former TN State Senator (R). Repeatedly praised Trump on social media. Subsequently went to work as a lawyer for the America First Policy Institute (Trump-aligned).
Donald Trump.summary: Former Tennessee state senator pardoned after he had begun serving a 21-month sentence for an illegal campaign finance scheme. He had been a vocal Trump supporter.
